data.title: Sandisk, Lumentum Rally Sparks Bearish Plays As Tradr Rolls Out 2X Short ETFs
data.description:
The timing of the launch is notable. Both LUMN and SNDK stocks have more than doubled in 2026, riding a powerful rally linked to the "physical layer" of artificial intelligence—where storage, optical networking, and chip testing are increasingly viewed as critical bottlenecks.

Key features of the new ETFs:

Tradr ETFs, which manages over $4 billion across 58 leveraged products, continues to expand its lineup as demand grows for high-conviction, short-term trading tools, especially in crowded AI trades where sharp upside moves can quickly invite equally aggressive reversals.

data.title: Private Equity Giant Blackstone's AUM Hits Record $1.3 Trillion
data.description:
Details

Distributable earnings per share rose to $1.36, up 25% from a year earlier and above the analyst consensus estimate of $1.35. Total distributable earnings increased 25% year over year to $1.76 billion.

Segment revenue climbed 24% to $3.433 billion, exceeding estimates of $3.397 billion. Fee-related earnings rose 23% to $1.55 billion. Within segments, fee-related earnings increased 13% in real estate, declined 4% in credit and insurance, surged 59% in private equity and rose 28% in multi-asset investing.

Net accrued performance revenue increased to $7.0 billion from $6.4 billion a year earlier.

Assets under management grew 12% to $1.30 trillion, supported by inflows of $68.5 billion during the quarter.

Dividend & Buyback

The company declared a quarterly dividend of $1.16 per share, payable May 11 to shareholders of record as of May 4, 2026. Blackstone also repurchased 0.2 million shares during the period.

As of March 31, 2026, the firm held $11.4 billion in total cash, cash equivalents, corporate treasury and other investments.

Management Commentary

Chief Executive Officer Stephen A. Schwarzman said the company delivered “outstanding first-quarter results despite the turbulent environment,” citing nearly $70 billion of inflows and broad-based appreciation across flagship strategies. He added that the firm’s “all-weather model” positions it to navigate disruption while deploying capital into attractive opportunities.

Earnings Call Highlights

Blackstone's management highlighted strong underlying momentum heading into 2026, with management fees outside real estate rising 15% year over year and continued scaling across key platforms like infrastructure and perpetual strategies.

Growth is expected to accelerate in the second half of the year, driven by new flagship funds (including private equity, energy transition, and strategic partners funds) coming out of fee holidays, though near-term fee growth will remain moderate.

The firm acknowledged headwinds in real estate and some deceleration in credit, but expects these to stabilize mid-year and improve thereafter.

Management emphasized the resilience of its capital-light model, which provides flexibility, minimal balance sheet risk, and consistent capital returns.

In retail channels, redemption activity has been concentrated among larger investors, while smaller investors remain sticky, and the firm sees potential to gain market share as weaker competitors exit.

On macro and geopolitical risks, including the Middle East conflict, Blackstone reported continued fundraising resilience and ongoing investment activity, supported by its diversified global base.

The firm is also actively managing AI-related risks across portfolios, with relatively limited exposure to vulnerable sectors and increased focus on adapting portfolio companies.

Longer term, management sees significant growth opportunities in private markets, including expansion into retirement channels like 401(k)s, where regulatory changes could unlock new investor access.

BX Price Action: Blackstone shares are down 5.28% at $122.88 during premarket trading on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: West Pharmaceutical Lifts Annual Guidance After Strong GLP-1 Driven Growth In Q1
data.description:
The injectable solutions company reported adjusted earnings of $2.13 per share, beating the consensus of $1.68.

Net sales of $844.90 million increased 21% year over year, with organic growth was 15.3%, beating the consensus of $779.97 million.

Proprietary Products sales of $694.3 million grew by 23.3% and increased 17.5% on an organic basis.

HVP components, which represents 48% of the company’s total net sales, continue to be the key growth driver.

Oral GLP-1 Trend Expands Market Opportunity

HVP components grew 23% on an organic basis, led by strong performance in both GLP-1 and GLP non-GLP1 revenues.

GLP1 revenues grew significantly and contributed 10% of total company sales.

The company also noted that though it is still early days in the adoption of orals, the trend is playing out as expected, and that oral GLP-1’s are expanding the market.

West Vantage Segment (Contract-Manufactured Products) sales reached $150.6 million, up 11.6% and 6.2% on an organic basis.

Management Highlights Strong Demand And Strategic Focus

The company’s strategic focus on biologics, GLP1s, Annex 1 conversions, and leveraging global infrastructure remains a priority for sustained growth.

Management expressed optimism about the future, despite macroeconomic uncertainties, and emphasized ongoing efforts to mitigate rising costs through operational efficiencies.

During the quarter, the board of directors authorized a new $1 billion share repurchase program, given the strong financial position.

West Pharmaceutical Raised Guidance Signals Continued Momentum

The company increased its fiscal 2026 guidance on excellent first-quarter results and expected continued momentum in business.

West Pharmaceutical raised its fiscal 2026 adjusted earnings per share guidance from $7.85-$8.20 to $8.40-$8.75 compared to the consensus of $8.01.

The company revised 2026 sales guidance from $3.22 billion-$3.28 billion to $3.29 billion-$3.35 billion, versus the Wall Street estimate of $3.25 billion.

WST Price Action: West Pharmaceutical shares were up 10.50% at $306.08 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: RLI Q1 2026 Earnings Call: Complete Transcript
data.description:
RLI (NYSE:RLI) held its first-quarter earnings conference call on Thursday. Below is the complete transcript from the call.

This transcript is brought to you by Benzinga APIs. For real-time access to our entire catalog, please visit https://www.benzinga.com/apis/ for a consultation.

Access the full call at https://events.q4inc.com/attendee/570395995

Summary

RLI reported a combined ratio of 86 for the quarter, with a 3% growth in premiums and a 15% increase in net investment income.

Operating earnings were $0.83 per share, slightly down from $0.89 last year, influenced by catastrophic activities and variability in insurance risks.

The casualty segment saw a 10% growth in premiums, driven by personal umbrella and commercial transportation, despite a competitive market environment.

RLI is focusing on strategic areas such as producer relationships, rate adequacy, and leveraging artificial intelligence to improve data and decision-making.

The company raised $300 million of long-term debt and received an A+ rating upgrade from AM Best, reflecting its strong financial position.

Future outlook remains positive, with expectations for continued growth and profitability despite market challenges, supported by disciplined underwriting and strategic investments.

Full Transcript

OPERATOR

Craig Kliethermus

Jen Klobnock (Chief Operating Officer)

OPERATOR

Michael Phillips (Equity Analyst)

Thanks. Good afternoon everybody. I'm curious how you would classify in your GL book the just overall competitive environment this quarter versus recent previous quarters.

Jen Klobnock (Chief Operating Officer)

Michael Phillips (Equity Analyst)

Okay, Jen, thank you. You mentioned in your earlier comments too about your plans for more state diversification in your personal umbrella book, any early impacts you've seen, you took a pretty big rate hike in California there. Any early impacts of what's happening in California from that?

Jen Klobnock (Chief Operating Officer)

Michael Phillips (Equity Analyst)

Okay, thank you, Jen. And then just lastly, you've talked for the last couple quarters about the transportation claim count information coming down. I guess just to play devil's advocate there, is there anything that would cause just more of a delay in the clean reporting and maybe pick up later, or is that truly a reduction on ultimate counts that you think could happen?

Jen Klobnock (Chief Operating Officer)

OPERATOR

Our next question comes from the line of Mark Hughes with Truist. Mark, please go ahead.

Mark Hughes (Equity Analyst)

Yeah, thank you. I wonder if you could talk a little bit about the property business. You were still down this quarter a little bit less than, than last quarter though I wonder, is the market still adjusting, which is to say pricing continues to decline sequentially. Is it at a point where maybe it might stabilize in the second half? How do you see that kind of near term trajectory?

Jen Klobnock (Chief Operating Officer)

Mark Hughes (Equity Analyst)

Jen Klobnock (Chief Operating Officer)

Yeah, that was specifically for our, our relatable construction business through the wholesalers. Yeah.

Mark Hughes (Equity Analyst)

Yeah. Okay. Thank you very much. Thank you.

OPERATOR

Our next call comes from the line of Andrew Anderson with Jefferies. Andrew, please go ahead.

Andrew Anderson (Equity Analyst)

Jen Klobnock (Chief Operating Officer)

Andrew Anderson (Equity Analyst)

Thank you.

OPERATOR

Our next call comes from the line of Greg Peters with Raymond James. Greg, please go ahead.

Mitch Rubin (Equity Analyst)

Hey, good afternoon, this is Mitch Rubin on for Greg in surety. With the large contract loss in the quarter, should we expect any further development on that claim in coming quarters or is the impact largely contained in this quarter? Thanks.

Aaron Diefenthaler (Chief Financial Officer)

I would say that we've reserved for basically the worst case scenario on that claim. So I wouldn't expect adverse development on that claim. And I would also just reiterate that that's a single claim. We don't see a systemic issue in the book. It was just one individual circumstance for a particular contractor.

Mitch Rubin (Equity Analyst)

Got it. Thank you for the answer there. And as a follow up, some peers have pointed to recent softening in financial lines. Are you seeing similar pressure in your executive products or professional services books?

Jen Klobnock (Chief Operating Officer)

Mitch Rubin (Equity Analyst)

Thank you.

OPERATOR

If there are no further questions, I will now turn the conference over to Mr. Craig Kliethermes for some closing remarks.

Craig Kliethermes (President and Chief Executive Officer)

OPERATOR

Our next Our next question comes from the line of Christian Getsoft from Wells Fargo Christian, please go ahead.

Christian Getsoft (Equity Analyst)

Hey, good afternoon. Thank you guys for fitting me in. I just had a question on the net retention and property that picked up five points. I just wanted to confirm that the uptick was purely reflective of lower reinsurance costs. And as we kind of get to the mid year renewals, are you guys thinking about any changes from a reinsurance strategy just given them the lower cost?

Aaron Diefenthaler (Chief Financial Officer)

Christian Getsoft (Equity Analyst)

Jen Klobnock (Chief Operating Officer)

We're seeing it a little bit on the casualty side, not to the extent of property. But we do see where for some of our contractors where we're being a little pickier on the auto coverage for some reason. Some standard markets say we like the GL and so we'll cover the auto as well. And we might lose it for that reason. I wouldn't call that a material impact on our book, but we're seeing that on the edges.

Christian Getsoft (Equity Analyst)

Great. Thank you.

OPERATOR

Our next call comes from the line of Meyer Shields from Keith Briet and Woods Meyer, please go ahead.

Meyer Shields

Aaron Diefenthaler (Chief Financial Officer)

In terms of reinsurance.

Meyer Shields

Jen Klobnock (Chief Operating Officer)

Meyer Shields

Okay, perfect. Thank you so much.

OPERATOR

Ladies and gentlemen, if you wish to access the replay for this call, you may do so on the RLI homepage at www.rlicorp.com. This concludes our conference for today. Thank you all for participating and have a nice day.

data.title: 9 Financials Stocks With Whale Alerts In Today's Session
data.description:
This whale alert can help traders discover the next big trading opportunities.

Whales are entities with large sums of money and we track their transactions here at Benzinga on our options activity scanner.

Traders will search for circumstances when the market estimation of an option diverges heavily from its normal worth. High amounts of trading activity could push option prices to exaggerated or underestimated levels.

Here's the list of options activity happening in today's session:

Explanation

These bullet-by-bullet explanations have been constructed using the accompanying table.

Options Alert Terminology
- Call Contracts: The right to buy shares as indicated in the contract.
- Put Contracts: The right to sell shares as indicated in the contract.
- Expiration Date: When the contract expires. One must act on the contract by this date if one wants to use it.
- Premium/Option Price: The price of the contract.
